location.formative: University of California, Santa Cruz — composition, gamelan, electronic music
location.career: New York City (downtown scene) → Seattle, WA (Capitol Hill, 1988-present)

examples: Gravitas Quartet — Way Out East (2006, Songlines): structurally complete compositions with designated improvisational openings
Gravitas Quartet — Some Places Are Forever Afternoon (2015): extended composed-improvisation forms
Zony Mash — structured grooves with eruptive improvisational sections
theory_nodes.description: Compositions that are structurally complete but contain designated spaces where improvisation is expected, invited, and bounded. The architectural plan specifies harmony, texture, dynamic arc, and timbral palette; the performers furnish the rooms. More detailed than a jazz lead sheet, less prescriptive than a full orchestral score.

genealogy.relationship: mediator — Jones composed everything (full scores), Frisell composes loosely (frameworks), Horvitz sits between: detailed structural plans with designated improvisational spaces

examples: Zony Mash — Brand Spankin' New (1998): bass in 4/4 against guitar riffs in 7/8
Zony Mash — Upper Egypt (2001): layered rhythmic cycles
theory_nodes.description: Multiple simultaneous time signatures operating in parallel. Individual parts are internally consistent; complexity emerges from interaction. Rhythmic polyphony that mirrors harmonic polyphony. Extends the rhythmic vocabulary beyond Jones's swing (regular pulse + syncopation) and Frisell's rubato (dissolved pulse) to multiplied pulse.

genealogy.relationship: new concept — first polymetric instance in S36 engine. Rhythmic spectrum: Jones (regular pulse) → Horvitz (multiplied pulse) → Frisell (dissolved pulse)

examples: Gravitas Quartet — piano passages with adjacent-second stacking
Some Places Are Forever Afternoon (2015) — middle-register density as gravitational anchor
Chamber works where clusters anchor free improvisation above and below
theory_nodes.description: Chords built from adjacent seconds (C-D-E or C-Db-D) creating dense harmonic texture. Bartok/Ligeti lineage deployed in jazz-adjacent contexts. Where Frisell leaves registers empty (implied polyphony), Horvitz fills the middle register with clusters (explicit density). Part of the density spectrum: Jones (maximum, all registers) → Horvitz (selective, middle register) → Frisell (minimum, registers empty).

genealogy.relationship: density spectrum midpoint — Jones fills all registers, Frisell empties them, Horvitz selectively fills the middle register

examples: Gravitas Quartet — Way Out East (2006): quartal piano voicings blending with cello and bassoon
Chamber works where stacked fourths produce open, ambiguous sonority
theory_nodes.description: Chords stacked in fourths (C-F-Bb-Eb) rather than traditional tertian stacking of thirds. Open, ambiguous sound — neither major nor minor, neither tense nor resolved. McCoy Tyner lineage via Coltrane quartet. Complements Frisell's modal ambiguity (degree 1) — both seek territory outside the major/minor binary through different voicing techniques.

genealogy.relationship: parallel approach — Frisell achieves ambiguity via wide intervals omitting the third; Horvitz achieves it via quartal stacking replacing the third with a fourth

examples: Zony Mash — Cold Spell (2005): electronics alongside Hammond organ and guitar
Live performances with real-time piano/organ signal processing
Film scoring with chamber-electronic hybrid textures
theory_nodes.description: Electronic processing as extension of acoustic instrument, not replacement. Piano or organ signal processed in real time — sampling, looping, effects — creating textures that blur the boundary between acoustic source and electronic transformation. Parallels Frisell's signal-chain-as-instrument approach from the keyboard rather than the guitar.

genealogy.relationship: technology-as-instrument advances — Jones (studio production), Frisell (guitar signal chain), Horvitz (keyboard signal chain)

examples: Gravitas Quartet extended compositions — continuous arc with improvisational eruptions
The Royal Room live performances — long-form structural arcs
Film scoring — dramatic narrative structure with performer freedom
theory_nodes.description: Longer works that proceed as a continuous arc with improvisation erupting at specific structural moments — springs breaking through rock. The form is planned; the content of those specific moments is not. Synthesizes Jones's film-score narrative technique (degree 0) with Frisell's improvisational fluency (degree 1).

genealogy.relationship: synthesis — Jones's through-composed narrative + Frisell's improvisational openness = Horvitz's through-composed form with designated improvisational eruptions

seattle_connection: Capitol Hill (1988-present). Co-founder of The Royal Room (Columbia City, 2012). Built the institutional infrastructure of Seattle's improvised music community. Co-migration with Frisell from NYC downtown scene. Married to Robin Holcomb (singer/composer). The artist-as-institution pattern.

theory_genealogy: Composed improvisation — first instance, mediates between Jones (full score) and Frisell (loose frameworks)
Polymetric structures — first instance, extends rhythmic vocabulary beyond swing and rubato
Cluster voicings — first instance, midpoint on density spectrum
Quartal voicings — first instance, parallel to Frisell's modal ambiguity
Electronic-acoustic integration — third instance (Jones studio, Frisell guitar, Horvitz keyboard)
Through-composed improvisation — first instance, synthesizes degrees 0 and 1
immediate_items: Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) inherits: Garfield High School lineage from degree 0, Jackson Street corridor as direct personal context, Seattle vocal jazz tradition, the original export pattern (local cultivation → global departure)
Anderson bridges back to the Jackson Street era that Jones represents — degree 3 should close the loop on the original export pattern and contrast it with The Seattle Draw of degrees 1-2

The artist-as-institution pattern should be tracked at degree 3: did Anderson build infrastructure, or was she purely a performer? The spectrum: Jones (industry), Horvitz (community), Frisell (none — pure performer). Where does Anderson fall?
The Garfield High School pipeline (Jones, Anderson, Hendrix) needs expansion at degree 3 — Anderson attended in the late 1940s, overlapping with Jones's era
Robin Holcomb as degree neighbor: if she appears in the S36 engine, the Horvitz-Holcomb creative partnership should be treated as a shared context module
pattern_items: The composition-improvisation spectrum now has three data points — continue tracking at every subsequent degree
The density spectrum (Jones/Horvitz/Frisell) should be extended to subsequent keyboard and ensemble artists
The Seattle Draw pattern is confirmed (Horvitz 1988, Frisell 1989, Holcomb 1988) — track all NYC-to-Seattle migrations
Cross-genre bridge-building: Jones (jazz→pop/R&B/film), Frisell (jazz→Americana/folk), Horvitz (jazz→funk/rock/chamber) — track the expanding territory of what jazz harmony can inhabit
The artist-as-institution pattern (Jones=industry, Horvitz=community) should be tracked across all 360 degrees — who builds infrastructure vs. who performs within it?
UC Santa Cruz appears as Horvitz's formative institution, alongside Berklee (Jones, Frisell). Track all institutional lineages.

key_albums.title: Some Order, Long Understood
key_albums.label: Sound Aspects
key_albums.significance: Early statement of composed improvisation. NYC downtown aesthetic.

key_albums.title: Brand Spankin' New
key_albums.label: Intuition
key_albums.significance: Zony Mash debut. Hammond organ grooves meet improvisational eruptions.

key_albums.title: Some Places Are Forever Afternoon
key_albums.label: National Sawdust Tracks
key_albums.significance: Late Gravitas statement. PNW landscape evoked through timbre and pacing.

key_projects.name: Zony Mash
key_projects.description: Funk/rock/improv group. Hammond organ, guitar, bass, drums.

key_projects.name: Gravitas Quartet
key_projects.description: Piano, cello, bassoon, cornet. Chamber-scale composed improvisation.

key_projects.name: The President
key_projects.description: Rock energy meets jazz harmony. NYC downtown formation.

key_projects.name: Pigpen
key_projects.description: With Frisell and Wollesen. Country, free jazz, atmospheric electronics.

key_projects.name: The Royal Room
key_projects.description: Performance venue in Columbia City. Co-founded by Horvitz. Anchor institution for Seattle creative music.

key_projects.name: New York Composers Orchestra
key_projects.description: Large ensemble for downtown composers. Co-founded by Horvitz. Institutional infrastructure for experimental music.
